initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason>Player BLUE is offering 60 ZUP, which is significantly above my cost of 40 ZUP. This is a very good offer and I should accept it. I've made two proposals already, and this is a favorable trade, so I will accept.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>Deal! 60 ZUP for the X sounds great. Thanks for the trade, Player BLUE!</message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All required tags are present in the correct order, and the content within each tag appears valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting the trade for 60 ZUP is excellent, exceeding my cost of 40 ZUP. There's no room for improvement here, as accepting is optimal.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent explicitly stated it was their final offer, and it’s a very good one for me. Rejecting would be irrational.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> clearly explains why I am accepting, aligning with the <player answer> and <newly proposed trade>. The <message> is also consistent with accepting the deal.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have only made two proposals and am now correctly responding with ACCEPT. I am respecting the turn constraints.
</critique>
